medications.indication_rationale: Antiplatelet therapy reduces cardiovascular events in symptomatic PAD and is appropriate to start (or arrange promptly), unless contraindicated. The source started aspirin 100 mg daily.
medications.dose_route_frequency: aspirin 100 mg orally once daily (as started in the source encounter)

medications.indication_rationale: Aggressive LDL lowering with a high-intensity statin is essential in symptomatic PAD/severe hyperlipidaemia to stabilise plaque and reduce MI/stroke risk; commenced after fasting lipids and baseline bloods, generally via the GP rather than blind over telehealth today.
medications.dose_route_frequency: high-intensity statin to be commenced after fasting lipids/baseline bloods (dose set by the prescriber - not fabricated here)
contraindications_in_this_case: Confirm no statin intolerance/interactions and check baseline liver function; commence via GP after bloods

allied_health_referrals.rationale: A structured/supervised exercise (walk-through-pain) programme promotes collateral formation and improves walking distance in claudication

integrative_alternative_therapies.therapy_name: Rest and activity avoidance as the treatment
integrative_alternative_therapies.rationale: Avoiding activity worsens deconditioning and does not treat PAD; a structured walking programme (not rest) plus medical therapy is the correct approach.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Begin a structured walking programme: walk to the point of moderate calf pain, rest until it fully resolves, then continue - building distance over time.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Repeated walk-through-pain cycles stimulate collateral vessel growth and progressively increase pain-free walking distance.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Reduce processed/high-fat foods and adopt a heart-healthy diet.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Dietary change supports LDL lowering and cardiovascular risk reduction alongside statin therapy.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Watch for and act immediately on limb-threatening changes - constant rest pain, or a pale, cold or numb foot.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Early recognition of acute limb ischaemia enables emergency treatment to save the limb.

patient_education_points.point: Your leg cramps on walking are from narrowed arteries (poor circulation), caused by high cholesterol building up as plaque. The good news is your leg is safe at rest right now - but this needs prompt proper assessment, not an emergency dash tonight.

Please arrange a prompt in-person vascular assessment (pulse checks and an ankle-pressure test), start the daily low-dose aspirin, get fasting bloods to start cholesterol-lowering treatment, and begin a structured walking programme.

Go to the emergency department immediately if your leg pain becomes constant even at rest, or your foot turns pale, cold or numb - those are warning signs the circulation has become critically blocked.
The same plaque affects your heart and brain, so cholesterol-lowering medication, aspirin, a better diet and regular walking are important to prevent a heart attack or stroke.

follow_up_plan.interval: Prompt in-person vascular assessment (urgent, within 24-48 hours); GP review in ~3 days for lipid results and to commence statin; vascular surgery/ABI referral
follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Pulse examination and ABI, fasting lipids/HbA1c/ECG results, initiation and tolerance of statin/antiplatelet, walking distance progress, and overall cardiovascular risk management
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Constant rest pain, or a pale/cold/numb foot - attend the emergency department immediately.

must_include_items: Recognition of stable claudication/PAD with a currently viable limb (not an emergency)
Prompt in-person vascular assessment (pulses/ABI) and cardiovascular risk treatment (antiplatelet + statin + risk-factor modification)
Structured walking programme rather than rest
Explicit ED safety-netting for acute limb ischaemia (rest pain; pale/cold/numb foot)
Avoidance of unnecessary emergency over-triage for a viable limb
acceptable_alternatives: Urgent same-day vascular assessment or within 24-48 hours are both acceptable given the acute worsening and telehealth exam limits
'Poor circulation/peripheral arterial disease', 'narrowed leg arteries from cholesterol' are acceptable framings
errors_of_omission: No structured exercise advice (or advising rest instead)
errors_of_commission: Over-triaging a viable limb to emergency surgery/ED unnecessarily
Reassuring without any follow-up or treatment ('just rest it')
Anchoring on DVT and arranging a leg ultrasound as the primary action
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: BALANCED-TRIAGE CASE: The AI Doctor must neither over-triage a viable limb to emergency nor under-treat. A pass requires (1) prompt vascular assessment and cardiovascular risk treatment AND (2) explicit acute-limb-ischaemia safety-netting. Reassuring with no plan, or sending a stable viable limb to ED as an emergency, are both failures.
